English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Clifford J. Earle papers
Earle, Clifford John, Jr.
14-19-4339
4 cubic feet. 4 cubic feet.
1953-2011. Correspondence, diaries, research material. Collection includes course material, correspondence, diaries, papers and other miscellaneous notes. Collection material in English 10470495
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Katie Guttman
Date completed:
October 2018
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, October 2018
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, November 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Clifford's research concerned invariants belonging to Riemann surfaces. He earned his Bachelor's degree from Swarthmore in 1957 and his Master's and Ph.D. degrees from Harvard in 1958 and in 1962 under the direction of Lars Ahlfors. His thesis was titled Teichmuand#x308;ller Spaces of Groups of the Second Kind. A paper of the same title was published in Acta Mathematica in 1964. From 1963 to 1965 he was a member of the Institute for Advanced Study in Princeton. In 1965 he became an Assistant Professor and in 1969 Full Professor at Cornell. From 1976 to 1979 he served as Chair of Mathematics. He was the first member of the Department to serve on the newly formed Faculty Senate in 1969. Cliff was a Guggenheim Fellow, an inaugural Fellow of the American Mathematical Society, a Distinguished Ordway Visitor at the University of Minnesota and Honorary Professor at the University of Warwick.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Collection includes course material, correspondence, diaries, papers and other miscellaneous notes.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Course Material
Series II. General Documents
